calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Pure-Play benchmark: >80% of program services spend is attributed to a single programme.

details.calculation: ($195,232 USD / 19,553 total beneficiaries) = $9.98 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 100 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'My gratitude for the timely care provided to 19553 animals, through life saving vaccinations, Veterinary interventions and critical care.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 0%, Wild Animals: 0%, Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Every loss was personal, reviewed and studied for valuable lessons, fortifying our resolve and resilience to do better.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

Blue Cross of Hyderabad is a registered non-profit animal welfare organization, recognized by the Animal Welfare Board of India since 1993.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total number of animals provided timely care through vaccinations, veterinary interventions, and critical care during 2024-25.

source.quote: My gratitude for the timely care provided to 19553 animals, through life saving vaccinations, Veterinary interventions and critical care.

metrics.context_qualifier: Community dogs and cats underwent Animal Birth Control and Anti-Rabies Vaccination programs.

source.quote: 11,662 ABC/ARV of community dogs and cats

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs sterilised and vaccinated in Medchal-Malkajgiri District as part of the ABC/ARV program.

source.quote: IMPACT: A total of 6425 dogs were sterilised ad vaccinated in Medchal-Malkajgiri District.

metrics.context_qualifier: Rabies and disease prevention vaccinations administered in various camps.
metrics.metric_name: Rabies and Disease Prevention Vaccination in Camps

source.quote: 6397 Rabies and disease prevention vaccination administered in camps

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs vaccinated during a successful camp on World Rabies Prevention Day with support from WVS and Mission Rabies.
metrics.metric_name: Dogs Vaccinated (World Rabies Prevention Day Camp)

source.quote: On the occasion of World Rabies Prevention Day, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a successful vaccination camp, where an impressive 4,415 dogs were vaccinated with the support of WVS and Mission Rabies over a fortnight leading to the event.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs vaccinated across the city during a large ARV/Canine Disease Vaccination Camp in collaboration with Mission Rabies and WVS.

source.quote: More than 4000 dogs were vaccinated across the city.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs sterilised and vaccinated in Rangareddy District as part of the ABC/ARV program.

source.quote: IMPACT: A total of 2947 dogs were sterilised ad vaccinated in Rangareddy District.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs sterilised and vaccinated in Hyderabad and Secunderabad Districts as part of the ABC/ARV program.

source.quote: IMPACT: A total of 1414 dogs were sterilised ad vaccinated in Hyderabad and Secunderabad Districts.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals received treatment in the pet clinic during the reporting year.

source.quote: 1225 Animals treated in pet clinic

metrics.context_qualifier: Street dogs geo-tagged and vaccinated in Manikonda during a large-scale community drive.

source.quote: A large-scale community geo-tagging, for the first time in Hyderabad and Anti-Rabies Vaccination drive was conducted in Manikonda, covering over 812 street dogs.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs sterilised and vaccinated in Sangareddy District as part of the ABC/ARV program.

source.quote: IMPACT: A total of 525 dogs were sterilised ad vaccinated in Sangareddy District.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs covered under a 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys in the Pocharam region.

source.quote: A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, was led by CSR team- Vamshi Muthyapu & Padmaja in Pocharam region. 500 dogs were covered in areas of Korremulla and Chowdariguda.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs vaccinated during a camp on World Zoonosis Day to raise awareness about zoonotic diseases.

source.quote: On World Zoonosis Day, Blue Cross of Hyderabad organized a vaccination camp, successfully vaccinating 355 dogs.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs sterilised and vaccinated in Tooparan District as part of the ABC/ARV program.

source.quote: IMPACT: A total of 212 dogs were sterilised ad vaccinated in Tooparan District.

metrics.context_qualifier: Assistance provided for emergency cases involving animals during the reporting year.

source.quote: 159 Assistance of emergency cases

metrics.context_qualifier: Homeless animals found forever homes through adoption drives during the reporting year.

source.quote: 110 Adoption of homeless animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs sterilised and vaccinated in Secunderabad Cantonment Board as part of the ABC/ARV program.

source.quote: IMPACT: A total of 104 dogs were sterilised ad vaccinated in Tooparan District.
The Blue Cross of Hyderabad systematically implements the Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-Rabies Vaccination (ARV) programs across multiple municipalities.

significant_events.summary: Systematically implemented Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-Rabies Vaccination (ARV) programs across multiple municipalities in Hyderabad and surrounding districts.

source.quote: During 2024-25 we have sterilised and vaccinated 11627 community dogs.

significant_events.summary: Sterilised and vaccinated 11,627 community dogs during the 2024-25 reporting year.

source.quote: With Rabies Free Telangana as the goal, a large vaccination drive was held in association with Mission Rabies and World Veterinary Services (WVS) collaborating with Blue Cross of Hyderabad. The campaign focused on rabies prevention and awareness and was timed with World Rabies Day to maximize impact. More than 4000 dogs were vaccinated across the city.

significant_events.summary: A large vaccination drive was held in association with Mission Rabies and WVS, vaccinating over 4000 dogs across the city from September 15 to 28, 2024.

significant_events.event_name: World Rabies Prevention Day Vaccination Camp

source.quote: On the occasion of World Rabies Prevention Day, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a successful vaccination camp, where an impressive 4,415 dogs were vaccinated with the support of WVS and Mission Rabies over a fortnight leading to the event.

significant_events.summary: Conducted a successful vaccination camp on September 28, 2024, where 4,415 dogs were vaccinated with support from WVS and Mission Rabies.

source.quote: March 29, 2025 - Geo-Tagging & Canine Vaccinations in Manikonda A large-scale community geo-tagging, for the first time in Hyderabad and Anti-Rabies Vaccination drive was conducted in Manikonda, covering over 812 street dogs.

significant_events.summary: Conducted a large-scale community geo-tagging and anti-rabies vaccination drive in Manikonda on March 29, 2025, covering over 812 street dogs.

source.quote: October 17 to February 4, 2024 Infosys ABC/ARV Project A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, was led by CSR team- Vamshi Muthyapu & Padmaja in Pocharam region. 500 dogs were covered in areas of Korremulla and Chowdariguda.

significant_events.summary: A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, covering 500 dogs in the Pocharam region from October 17, 2024, to February 4, 2025.

source.quote: July 6, 2024 - World Zoonosis Day On World Zoonosis Day, Blue Cross of Hyderabad organized a vaccination camp, successfully vaccinating 355 dogs.

significant_events.summary: Organized a vaccination camp on July 6, 2024, successfully vaccinating 355 dogs and raising awareness about zoonotic diseases.

source.quote: September 30 to October 25, 2024 - ABC Veterinary Training was conducted Blue Cross of Hyderabad and Worldwide Veterinary Service conducted specialized ABC surgery training for two batches of Government Vets, training 16 veterinarians in modern welfare practices for street dogs.

significant_events.summary: Conducted specialized ABC surgery training for 16 government veterinarians in modern welfare practices for street dogs from September 30 to October 25, 2024.

source.quote: February 19th to 28, 2025 - Training with Worldwide Veterinary Service The Blue Cross team travelled to Goa for further training under WVS. They gained hands-on experience in welfare interventions.

significant_events.summary: Blue Cross team travelled to Goa from February 19th to 28, 2025, for further training under WVS, gaining hands-on experience in welfare interventions.

source.quote: January 29 to February 4, 2025 WVS Training (Mumbai) The ABC team of Blue cross of Hyderabad was selected for WVS advanced training in Mumbai.

significant_events.summary: ABC team selected for WVS advanced training in Mumbai from January 29 to February 4, 2025, focusing on vaccination and field skills enhancement.

source.quote: July 24, 2024- Toopran ABC/ARV Project Launched Blue Cross of Hyderabad begins operations under the Toopran Project initiative.

significant_events.summary: Launched operations under the Toopran Project initiative on July 24, 2024, to address street animal population and welfare through ABC/ARV.

source.quote: August 2, 2024 Shankarpally ABC/ARV Project launched Launching the second project in Shankarpally municipality, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted sterilization, vaccination, and outreach work, broadening our footprint in suburban areas that need attention.

significant_events.summary: Launched the second project in Shankarpally municipality on August 2, 2024, conducting sterilization, vaccination, and outreach work.

source.quote: December 1, 2024 - New ABC/ARV Project commences at Cantonment Blue Cross of Hyderabad launched a new Animal Birth Control (ABC) project at the Cantonment area on December 1st, 2024.

significant_events.summary: Launched a new Animal Birth Control (ABC) project at the Cantonment area on December 1, 2024, to humanely manage street dog population.

source.quote: Adoptions are organized on a dedicated Adoption Day held on the last Sunday of every month, helping to find loving homes for these young animals and reducing the number of strays on streets.

significant_events.summary: Organized monthly adoption days for puppies rescued with their mothers during ABC programs, finding loving homes for young animals.

source.quote: April 27, 2024 - Radio Mirchi conducted a Hitech Adoption Drive Radio Mirchi teamed up with Blue Cross of Hyderabad for a large-scale adoption drive drawing attention to shelter animals who need homes.

significant_events.summary: Teamed up with Radio Mirchi on April 27, 2024, for a large-scale adoption drive, connecting adopters with well-cared-for vaccinated pets.

source.quote: January 25, 2025 – "Love my Indie Dog" Show Our much awaited annual event celebrating native Indian dogs, organized by Blue Cross of Hyderabad, hosted by Chaitanya Vidyalaya and prizes sponsored by MARS Petcare.

significant_events.summary: Organized a heartwarming event on January 25, 2025, celebrating Indian street dogs, promoting adoption, and encouraging responsible pet ownership.

significant_events.event_name: Secunderabad Awareness Drive & Water Bowl Distribution

source.quote: As part of our community outreach initiative, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a series of awareness programs across the Secunderabad region. The goal was to sensitize the public on animal welfare issues, especially during the summer months when street animals struggle to find clean drinking water.

significant_events.summary: Conducted awareness programs and distributed water bowls across Secunderabad to sensitize the public on animal welfare.

source.quote: We conducted awareness sessions in schools to educate students on responsible pet care, rabies prevention, and the importance of animal birth control.

significant_events.summary: Conducted awareness sessions in Narsingi Government Schools to educate students on responsible pet care, rabies prevention, and ABC.

source.quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted an awareness camp at Gokaraju Rangaraju College, focusing on key issues such as animal welfare, zoonotic diseases, vaccinations, and the ABC/ARV programs.

significant_events.summary: Conducted an awareness camp at Gokaraju Rangaraju College focusing on animal welfare, zoonotic diseases, vaccinations, and ABC/ARV programs.

source.quote: September 28, 2024 - Rabies Prevention Day Awareness Rally A public rally was conducted from Blue Cross of Hyderabad to KBR Park with the goal to spread awareness on animal welfare and health.

significant_events.summary: A public rally was conducted on September 28, 2024, from Blue Cross of Hyderabad to KBR Park to spread awareness on animal welfare and health.

source.quote: Every loss was personal, reviewed and studied for valuable lessons, fortifying our resolve and resilience to do better.

data.original_text: Blue Cross of Hyderabad is a non-profit organization dedicated to street animal welfare.
Their mission is to focus on animal birth control, vaccinations, and animal welfare training.
They work in alliance with the GHMC to implement the Animal Birth Control Anti-Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in specific areas of the city.
The organization also provides basic checkups, vaccinations, and emergency care for sick and injured animals.
They offer training for volunteers, veterinarians, pet owners, and animal welfare groups.
Adoption events are held monthly to facilitate the adoption of healthy puppies and kittens.
The organization does not accept abandoned animals or house pets for boarding and encourages the use of government veterinary hospitals and GHMC animal shelters.
To support their cause, visitors can donate, sponsor, volunteer, or become a member through their official website.
Located in Hyderabad, Telangana, the Blue Cross of Hyderabad operates from a two-acre animal shelter in Jubilee Hills.
The shelter is surrounded by hills and vast open spaces, housing over 650 animals of different species.

The organisation prevents cruelty to animals, rescues the sick and the injured, shelters them, provides mobile clinics, educates people on animal welfare and carries out many more animal welfare activities that upholds the existence of these simple souls.
To reduce the number of homeless stray animals in the streets, Blue Cross vaccinates over 15,000 stray dogs, against rabies, each year.
The organisation sterilises around 600 street dogs and cats every month and vaccinates them against rabies.
It also conducts extensive adoption drives to find homes for stray puppies and kittens to reduce the number of homeless strays in the streets.
Many who semi own dogs but cannot afford Veterinary treatment, become allies in the dog management of the area.

Blue Cross aims to reach a large target audience within the community so they support the Animal Birth Control/Anti-Rabies Vaccination efforts, how to behave around animals and thereby create safe communities.
Our Animal Welfare Training Centre facilitates training for Veterinarians, pet owners, animal welfare groups and students.
We conduct an effective Animal Birth Control/ Anti Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in West Zone, Jubilee and Banjara Hills, 15 kms around our location.
In 2016, BCH trained 114 government vets from Andhra Pradesh, organising a three-week training program-me that was conducted in six batches.

The annual report summarizes the activities of Blue Cross of Hyderabad for 2019-2020. It highlights that 11,409 animals were helped over the year, including 9,708 that underwent animal birth control operations.

During the financial year of 2018-19, Blue Cross of Hyderabad was able to provide help to 10,844 animals; of which 8198 were through the Animal Birth Control /Anti Rabies Vaccination programme, and the rest were treated in emergency care, at our clinic and through our adoption drives.
Our effort proved to be extremely successful to reduce stray dog population on the street and thereby reduce animal suffering exponentially.

We proved this by actually seeing a reduction in animals in accidents, less puppies dying of parvo and distemper, and more people helping animals; while ABC operated dogs are being accepted by their communities in the areas we work.
This mature and responsible attitude has boosted the number of people and groups helping animals to a new record.
Since inception through services and efforts, Blue Cross of Hyderabad has helped over 5,35,790 animals.

The pandemic year was completely handled by Mrs.kumari , our CFO and Our Veterinarians Dr.Shubam, Dr.Haritha, Dr.Irshad, supported by their teams of dedicated staff. We stayed focused on Animal Birth Control and Rabies control efforts.
The impressive numbers of animals helped, 38,640 to be precise, are due to a well coordinated team effort established over the years.
To highlight the work of our ambulance and rescue service, 8500 animals were rescued, sick, injured and a bused from the streets.
